text: An AI receptionist is only useful if the business owner can set it up in ten minutes without help. Everything else is a demo.
This is why I stopped recommending the web app to clients. The API stays consistent. The web layer is tuned for cost in the background with no changelog. Two products under one name.
Exit interview as a deployment artifact is a sharp framing. Small businesses lose more to a single resignation than to any tech gap. An agent built from that interview is defensible IP.
Charge for the outcome, not the prompt. Owners will not pay for hours of thinking. They will pay a flat monthly fee for an agent that answers calls and books jobs. The thinking goes in the setup fee.
